title: Clean Architecture
objectives: Explain the dependency rule and its layers
Test business logic without the UI or a network

sections.markdown: Clean Architecture (Uncle Bob) organizes code into concentric layers where **dependencies point inward**. Framework details (UI, DB, network) sit on the outside; pure business rules sit at the center.

sections.code:
[ Frameworks & Drivers ]     (Flutter, Retrofit, Room)
     [ Interface Adapters ]    (repositories, mappers, VMs)
        [ Use Cases ]          (application-specific business rules)
           [ Entities ]        (enterprise/pure business rules)
    dependencies ONLY point INWARD

sections.markdown: The dependency rule = the killer idea. Business rules never import Flutter/Retrofit/Hive. They depend on abstractions (interfaces) that the outer layers implement — Dependency Inversion in the large. Result: swap the UI, DB, or network without rewriting logic, and test it without any of them.

Building 'Clean' as three folders that all import Flutter and each other. Real Clean means the domain layer has **zero imports of framework code**. If it uses BuildContext or `dart:io`, it's not clean.
Don't overuse it. Small apps drown in boilerplate. Apply Clean where the payoff is real: long-lived apps, multiple data sources, strict testability, or teams larger than one.

flashcards.back: Source code dependencies point only inward — outer layers know inner ones, never the reverse.

flashcards.front: What lives in the innermost layer?
flashcards.back: Entities — pure domain models with no framework code.

flashcards.front: How does DI enable Clean Architecture?
flashcards.back: Domain defines interfaces; outer layers implement and inject them, so business rules stay framework-free.

quiz.q: The core principle of Clean Architecture is…
quiz.explain: Outer layers depend on inner ones, never the reverse.

quiz.q: Where do pure business rules live?
quiz.explain: Use cases and entities are the framework-free core.

quiz.q: Which import in a domain UseCase would violate Clean?
quiz.explain: Domain must not depend on Flutter or any framework.

challenges.text: Extract a domain Entity + UseCase from a widget's business logic.
Define a Repository interface in domain and implement it in data with a fake source.
Refactor a screen so its ViewModel only calls use cases; unit-test them without Flutter.
Add a second data source (e.g., cache) behind the same repository interface with no domain changes.

interviewQuestions: What is Clean Architecture's dependency rule?
Name the layers and what belongs in each.
How does DIP/DI make it work?
When is Clean Architecture worth the boilerplate?
How do you test business logic without the UI?
